metadata.title: Guidance for Industry: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior: Prospective Assessment of Occurrence in Clinical Trials

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must prospectively assess the occurrence of treatment-emergent suicidal ideation and behavior in clinical trials of drug and biological products by actively querying patients rather than relying on spontaneous reports.
rules.source_section: Section I: Introduction
rules.verbatim_quote: The principles discussed in this guidance for the prospective assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior involve actively querying patients about the occurrence of suicidal thinking and behavior, rather than relying on patients to report such occurrences spontaneously, followed by retrospective classification of events into appropriate categories.
rules.exceptions: Single-dose trials in healthy volunteers and microdose trials are exempt.

rules.rule_text: Suicidal ideation and behavior assessment instruments must classify events into the 11 preferred categories: 5 levels of suicidal ideation (passive, active nonspecific, active with method only, active with method and intent, active with method/intent/plan), 5 levels of suicidal behavior (completed suicide, suicide attempt, interrupted attempt, aborted attempt, preparatory actions), and self-injurious behavior with no suicidal intent.
rules.source_section: Section III.A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Assessment Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: the current preferred terms that we consider important include five levels of suicidal ideation, five levels of suicidal behavior, and the category self-injurious behavior, no suicidal intent. We have adopted these 11 categories as the standard for classifying suicidal ideation and behavior. ... Suicidal ideation: 1. Passive; 2. Active: Nonspecific (no method, intent, or plan); 3. Active: Method, but no intent or plan; 4. Active: Method and intent, but no plan; 5. Active: Method, intent, and plan. Suicidal behavior: 1. Completed suicide; 2. Suicide attempt; 3. Interrupted attempt; 4. Aborted attempt; 5. Preparatory actions toward imminent suicidal behaviors. Self-injurious behavior, no suicidal intent

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must use an assessment instrument that directly classifies suicidal ideation and behavior into the 11 preferred categories defined in Appendix A; the Columbia-Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) is an acceptable instrument for this purpose.
rules.source_section: Section III.A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Assessment Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend use of a suicidal ideation and behavior assessment instrument that directly classifies suicidal ideation and behavior into the 11 preferred categories, defined in Appendix A. As stated above, the C-SSRS is a prospective assessment instrument that directly classifies suicidal ideation and behavior into these 11 preferred categories, and this instrument would be acceptable for the purpose of these studies.
rules.exceptions: Sponsors should discuss the acceptability of alternative instruments with FDA before using them in clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: The suicidal ideation and behavior assessment instrument must include all categories of suicidal ideation and behavior identified in the 11 preferred terms, definitions for all categories, probes or questions permitting determination of whether each idea or behavior occurred, a mechanism for integration of information from other sources, and direct classification into the 11 preferred terms.
rules.source_section: Section III.A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Assessment Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: Categories: The instrument ideally should include all the categories of suicidal ideation and behavior identified in the 11 preferred terms defined in Appendix A. Definitions: The instrument should include definitions for all of these categories (these definitions ideally should coincide with the definitions in Appendix A). Probes/Questions: The instrument should include probes or questions that permit determination of whether or not each of these ideas or behaviors occurred. Other information: The instrument should provide for integration of information from other sources (e.g., family, friends, or significant others; caregivers or health professionals; hospital or emergency room records; coroner's report or death certificate) to permit accurate completion of the assessment. Direct classification into the 11 preferred terms (see Appendix A): Use of the C-SSRS instrument accomplishes this goal directly
rules.exceptions: Instruments that do not accomplish direct classification can still be useful for patient protection in a trial but may not be usable in future meta-analyses.

rules.rule_text: There must be provisions for formal training of raters to ensure accuracy and consistency in application of the suicidal ideation and behavior assessment instrument.
rules.source_section: Section III.A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Assessment Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: Training: There should be provisions for formal training of raters to ensure accuracy and consistency in application of the instrument.

rules.rule_text: The C-SSRS form is not complete until all available relevant data have been accessed and integrated into the assessment; data entries for C-SSRS classified events must then serve as the basis for analyses of future trials focused on suicidal ideation and behavior.
rules.source_section: Section III.A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Assessment Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: It is important to note that the C-SSRS form is not complete until all available relevant data have been accessed and integrated into the assessment. Data entries for C-SSRS classified events then become the basis for analyses of future trials focused on suicidal ideation and behavior.

rules.rule_text: All discrete suicidal ideation or behavior events that can be determined must be separately classified and recorded for each reporting interval, including where a patient may have experienced multiple event types during the same interval.
rules.source_section: Section III.B: Managing Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Data
rules.verbatim_quote: Consequently, all events that can be determined to be discrete events should be separately classified and recorded for the interval being assessed.
rules.exceptions: When events represent a continuum of related events, it may be most reasonable to classify the continuum according to the most serious event.

rules.rule_text: Future meta-analyses for suicidal ideation and behavior should consider suicidal behavior and ideation separately, because they may have different predictive value for subsequent suicidal behavior.
rules.source_section: Section III.B: Managing Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Data
rules.verbatim_quote: it is likely that future meta-analyses will consider suicidal behavior and ideation separately, because they may have different predictive value for subsequent suicidal behavior.

rules.rule_text: Suicidal ideation and behavior should be assessed in every trial after it has been determined that the drug is appropriate for this assessment; the full assessment should involve a pooled analysis of all controlled trials so that a substantial database is available.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.1: Identifying Trials in Which Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Assessment Should be Carried Out
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, suicidal ideation and behavior should be assessed in every trial after it has been determined that the drug is appropriate for this assessment (see section IV.). The full assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior generally should involve a pooled analysis of all controlled trials, so that it will not be possible to conclude that a drug has no effect on suicidal ideation and behavior until a substantial database is available for this analysis.

rules.rule_text: It is permissible to omit or use alternative suicidal ideation and behavior assessments in trials involving patients with cognitive impairment so substantial as to interfere with understanding the concept of suicide, including severe Alzheimer's disease, severe cognitive impairment, other dementias, mental retardation, and autism; sponsors must obtain prior agreement with the review division before omitting such assessments.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.2: Populations in Which Assessment of Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Would be Difficult
rules.verbatim_quote: It is reasonable to omit, or consider alternative assessments in, trials involving patients with cognitive impairment so substantial as to interfere with an understanding of the concept of suicide. Such populations can include certain patients with Alzheimer's disease (those with severe cognitive impairment), other dementias, mental retardation, and autism. ... A sponsor considering the omission of standard suicidal ideation and behavior assessments ... from a specific clinical trial in a particularly challenging population should discuss this omission with the review division to obtain prior agreement.
rules.exceptions: Critically ill patients would also be difficult to assess for suicidal ideation and behavior. Young children who have not reached sufficient cognitive maturity to understand the concept of death may also be exempted.

rules.rule_text: Multiple-dose trials in healthy volunteers must include suicidal ideation and behavior assessments; single-dose trials in healthy volunteers may omit such assessments.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.3a: Single-dose trials
rules.verbatim_quote: Therefore, we have concluded that multiple-dose trials in healthy volunteers should include such assessments, but that it is reasonable to omit such assessments from single-dose trials in healthy volunteers.
rules.exceptions: Single-dose trials in healthy volunteers may omit assessments because such trials are conducted in well-controlled settings with almost continuous observation.

rules.rule_text: Suicidal ideation and behavior assessments may be omitted from microdose trials involving low doses that are not expected to have any measurable pharmacological effects.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.3b: Microdose trials
rules.verbatim_quote: It is reasonable to omit suicidal ideation and behavior assessments in microdose trials involving low doses that are not expected to have any measurable pharmacological effects. Microdose trials are typically employed for imaging agents in the assessment of receptor occupancy.

rules.rule_text: In outpatient trials where suicidal ideation and behavior assessments are appropriate, assessments must be conducted at baseline (lifetime suicidal ideation and behavior assessment) and at all planned visits at which other clinical assessments are carried out.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.4: Timing of Assessments
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, in outpatient trials for which assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior are considered appropriate, assessments should be conducted at baseline (the lifetime suicidal ideation and behavior assessment) and at all planned visits at which other clinical assessments are to be carried out.
rules.exceptions: For inpatient trials, suicidal ideation and behavior assessments would not be needed at the times of nonsymptom assessments (e.g., vital signs).

rules.rule_text: For drugs with particularly long elimination half-lives, suicidal ideation and behavior assessments must include follow-up assessments even after dosing has stopped.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.4: Timing of Assessments
rules.verbatim_quote: For certain drugs (e.g., those with particularly long elimination half-lives), it may make sense to include follow-up assessments even after dosing has stopped.

rules.rule_text: Suicidal ideation and behavior assessments must also be conducted at any unplanned visits at which other clinical assessments are needed.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.4: Timing of Assessments
rules.verbatim_quote: These assessments should also be conducted at any unplanned visits at which other clinical assessments are needed.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must discuss with FDA how to implement suicidal ideation and behavior assessments in ongoing trials; suicidal ideation and behavior data derived from a trial where assessments were added after the trial was well along would not be optimal for inclusion in a meta-analysis.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.5: Implementation During Ongoing Trials
rules.verbatim_quote: Determining how to implement suicidal ideation and behavior assessments in ongoing trials may involve some discussion with the FDA. Suicidal ideation and behavior data derived from a trial in which suicidal ideation and behavior assessments were added after the trial was well along would not be optimal for inclusion in a meta-analysis.

rules.rule_text: Prospective suicidal ideation and behavior assessments must be carried out in all clinical trials involving any drug being developed for any psychiatric indication, as well as for all antiepileptic drugs and other neurologic drugs with CNS activity, both inpatient and outpatient, including multiple-dose phase 1 trials in healthy volunteers.
rules.source_section: Section IV: Prospective Assessment - Specific Indication Recommendations
rules.verbatim_quote: other than the exceptions noted in section III.C., prospective suicidal ideation and behavior assessments should be carried out in all clinical trials involving any drug being developed for any psychiatric indication, as well as for all antiepileptic drugs and other neurologic drugs with central nervous system (CNS) activity, both inpatient and outpatient, including multiple-dose phase 1 trials involving healthy volunteers.
rules.exceptions: Exceptions noted in Section III.C. apply (single-dose healthy volunteer trials, microdose trials, populations with severe cognitive impairment).

rules.rule_text: At a minimum, prospective suicidal ideation and behavior assessments must be carried out in all clinical trials for all drugs that are pharmacologically similar to drugs known to be associated with suicidal ideation and behavior signals, including isotretinoin and other tretinoins, beta blockers (especially those entering the brain), reserpine, smoking cessation drugs, and drugs for weight loss.
rules.source_section: Section IV: Prospective Assessment - Specific Indication Recommendations
rules.verbatim_quote: at a minimum, we recommend that prospective suicidal ideation and behavior assessments be carried out in all clinical trials for all drugs that are pharmacologically similar to drugs in the above list. These assessments, however, might reasonably be used more broadly, perhaps with any drug that appears to have a CNS effect. ... there has been long-standing concern about a variety of drugs, including isotretinoin and other tretinoins, beta blockers (especially those entering the brain), reserpine, drugs for smoking cessation, and drugs for weight loss, for which possible signals of risk for suicidal ideation and behavior have already been identified.

rules.rule_text: Questions about whether a particular drug under development would need assessments for suicidal ideation and behavior must be directed to the review division that has responsibility for the indication in question.
rules.source_section: Section IV: Prospective Assessment - Specific Indication Recommendations
rules.verbatim_quote: Questions about whether a particular drug under development would need assessments for suicidal ideation and behavior should be directed to the review division that has responsibility for the indication in question.

rules.rule_text: Passive suicidal ideation is defined as patient having thoughts about a wish to be dead or not alive anymore, or a wish to fall asleep and not wake up, and must be captured in any prospective assessment instrument.
rules.source_section: Appendix A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Categories and Definitions
rules.verbatim_quote: Passive suicidal ideation: wish to be dead. Patient has thoughts about a wish to be dead or not alive anymore, or wish to fall asleep and not wake up.

rules.rule_text: A completed suicide must be defined as a self-injurious behavior that resulted in fatality associated with at least some intent to die; evidence of intent to kill oneself can be explicit or inferred from the behavior or circumstance.
rules.source_section: Appendix A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Categories and Definitions
rules.verbatim_quote: Completed suicide: A self-injurious behavior that resulted in fatality and was associated with at least some intent to die as a result of the act. Evidence that the individual intended to kill him- or herself, at least to some degree, can be explicit or inferred from the behavior or circumstance.

rules.rule_text: Self-injurious behavior with no suicidal intent must be captured separately from suicidal behavior and defined as self-injurious behavior intended purely for other reasons (e.g., to relieve distress, self-mutilation, or to effect change in others) with no intent to die.
rules.source_section: Appendix A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Categories and Definitions
rules.verbatim_quote: Self-Injurious Behavior Without Suicidal Intent: Self-injurious behavior associated with no intent to die. The behavior is intended purely for other reasons, either to relieve distress (often referred to as self-mutilation (e.g., superficial cuts or scratches, hitting or banging, or burns)) or to effect change in others or the environment.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must use a consistent set of assessment instruments across development programs to maximize the opportunity to identify potential signals in future meta-analyses; use of different instruments across programs increases measurement variability and decreases opportunity to identify signals.
rules.source_section: Section III.A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Assessment Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: Although alternative instruments could be acceptable, it should be noted that the use of different assessment instruments in different programs is likely to increase measurement variability across programs, decreasing the opportunity to identify potential signals in future meta-analyses that include data from multiple programs. This type of imprecision is particularly problematic in dealing with events that have a low incidence, as is the case for suicidal ideation and behavior occurring in clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: Assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior must be conducted both inpatient and outpatient, and in multiple-dose phase 1 trials involving healthy volunteers, for drugs requiring this assessment.
rules.source_section: Section IV: Prospective Assessment - Specific Indication Recommendations
rules.verbatim_quote: Assessments should be conducted in both inpatient and outpatient trials, and even multiple-dose phase 1 trials involving healthy volunteers, with the exceptions noted in section III.C.
rules.exceptions: Exceptions noted in Section III.C. apply.

rules.rule_text: For trials where suicidal ideation and behavior assessments are added to an ongoing trial that is well along, sponsors should alert investigative sites of the general concern about possible drug-induced suicidal ideation and behavior so each site can individually decide how to address the issue.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.5: Implementation During Ongoing Trials
rules.verbatim_quote: Nevertheless, even in these instances, it may be useful to alert investigative sites of the general concern about possible drug-induced suicidal ideation and behavior, so they can individually decide how to address this issue.

rules.rule_text: For trials of drugs for psychiatric conditions, suicidal ideation and behavior assessments must be a regular part of development programs; there must be no assumption that patients with nondepressed psychiatric disorders have lesser vulnerability to treatment-induced suicidal ideation and behavior than patients with depression.
rules.source_section: Section IV: Prospective Assessment - Specific Indication Recommendations
rules.verbatim_quote: Past experience specifically indicates that assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior should be a regular part of development programs involving antidepressants and antiepileptic drugs. But the heightened risk of suicide in most psychiatric illnesses strongly suggests that suicidal ideation and behavior should be assessed as part of the evaluation of any drug being developed for a psychiatric condition. ... There are no data to support the view that patients with nondepressed psychiatric disorders have any lesser vulnerability to treatment-induced suicidal ideation and behavior than patients with overt depression.

rules.rule_text: The C-SSRS screening questions must be completed at baseline and at every patient visit; the full C-SSRS interview is required only when initial screening questions are positive.
rules.source_section: Section III.A: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior Assessment Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: The C-SSRS is a detailed interview, but the full interview is needed only if the initial screening questions about suicidal ideation and behavior are positive. Although the screening questions should be completed at baseline and at every visit for every patient, they are not by themselves burdensome, typically taking only 1 to 2 minutes for patients who have no positive findings.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors considering any drug for a nonpsychiatric indication that appears to have CNS activity should contact the relevant review division to discuss whether suicidal ideation and behavior assessments are recommended for that individual drug.
rules.source_section: Section IV: Prospective Assessment - Specific Indication Recommendations
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors are encouraged to contact the relevant review division to discuss whether these assessments are recommended for an individual drug. ... These assessments, however, might reasonably be used more broadly, perhaps with any drug that appears to have a CNS effect.
